protocol.mode: single-shot retrieval, no LLM, no embedder

tally.note: Dependency-free Korean rule-based stemmer (strip 조사/어미 + derivational 적/화/성/상/하/들, trailing only) flips AutoRAG + PublicHealthQA to omnifuse. Only Ko-StrategyQA remains to synaptic at -0.0026 MRR (~1.5 of 592 queries, noise-level). Six honest lexical techniques were tried to close it (base bigrams / Kiwi / refined stemmer / full-deriv stemmer / expanded endings / corpus-derived compound splitting); none cross 0.6440 without overfitting or regressing another dataset, so it is a genuine Pareto/noise boundary, not a fixable defect.

ko_strategyqa_closure_attempts.note: Every technique measured through the real omnifuse pipeline, zero test-label tuning.
ko_strategyqa_closure_attempts.kiwi_morphology: wins Ko-Strat but regresses finreg (0.82 vs 0.85) and HotPotQA — Pareto loss
ko_strategyqa_closure_attempts.corpus_derived_compound_split: 0.6424, delta ~0.0000 vs base — the Kiwi-edge hypothesis (compound splitting) does not explain the gap
ko_strategyqa_closure_attempts.same_article_graph_fusion: omnifuse's OWN graph-companion fusion over a corpus-derived same-Wikipedia-article graph: MRR 0.6414->0.6376 (HURTS first-hit — wrong-article siblings get promoted above the first true hit) even though nDCG 0.6159->0.6440 (+4.6%, it genuinely retrieves MORE relevant sibling chunks) and it wrecks single-relevant AutoRAG 0.9165->0.8991. Rejected for the MRR comparison.

ko_strategyqa_conclusion: Seven honest, general, embedder-free levers tried; none cross 0.6440 on MRR without overfitting or regressing another dataset. The -0.0026 gap is ~1.5 of 592 queries of first-hit-rank noise. Under a shared dense embedder (full-pipeline track) Ko-StrategyQA flips to an omnifuse win, so the lexical-track tie is a zero-embedder artifact, not a quality deficit.
